# Figure description: Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_7.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_7.jpg`

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite stratigraphic/lithological borehole correlation diagram** (геологический разрез / литологический разрез) — a multi-column well log correlation panel showing side-by-side lithological columns from multiple boreholes drilled in the same area. This is a classic Soviet-era "geological section" (геологический разрез) used to correlate stratigraphy between wells across a field or survey block.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers the **Мариновский (Marinovsky) deposit/area** in northern Kazakhstan, as indicated by repeated references in the column headers to "по скважинам борта Мариновского поднятия" (by boreholes of the flank of the Marinovsky uplift/structure). The diagram shows **at least 5–6 individual borehole lithological columns** arranged side by side for lateral correlation purposes.

Visible borehole/well references and header text suggest the following groupings:

- **Leftmost column(s):** Described as a geological section "по скважинам северо-северо-запада Мариновского поднятия" or similar — скважины (boreholes) referenced include what appear to be numbers in the **Д-40, 50-е, 60-е** series [partially illegible]
- **Central columns:** Headers reference "Скважины Н-225, 229, 206" and similar numbering [partially legible]
- **Right-side columns:** Reference "Скважины Н-629-632" or similar series [partially legible]
- A stamp visible in the lower right quadrant reads **"ФОНДЫ СССР Азербайджанский [illegible]"** and a fund number **"№ 36[2?]..."** — consistent with Report 36572

Depth ranges appear to extend from surface to approximately **400–500 m** based on scale markings visible along column margins, though precise depth tick values are largely illegible at this scan resolution.

---

## Key Content

### Column Structure
Each borehole is represented as a **vertical lithological strip** using standard Soviet geological hatching conventions:

- **Dotted/granular pattern** — indicates **sandstone** (песчаник) or coarse clastic sediments
- **Brick/horizontal line pattern** — indicates **limestone or carbonate rock** (известняк)
- **Dashed/wavy horizontal lines** — indicates **clay or mudstone** (глина/аргиллит)
- **Interlocked cross-hatch** — possibly **dolomite or anhydrite**
- **Blank/white sections** — possibly **salt, evaporite, or core loss**

### Stratigraphic Subdivision
The columns are subdivided by **stratigraphic index labels** placed to the left of each column. These appear to follow the standard Soviet Carboniferous/Permian stratigraphic notation:

Visible stratigraphic symbols include (left column, top to bottom):
- **C₁** or **C₁β** — Lower Carboniferous (Visean?)
- **C₁t** — Tournaisian Stage
- **C₂** — Middle Carboniferous (Bashkirian/Moscovian)
- **C₂b** — Bashkirian
- **C₂m** — Moscovian
- **C₃** — Upper Carboniferous (Gzhelian/Kasimovian?)
- **P₁** — Lower Permian
- **P₁a** — Asselian
- **K** or **Kz** labels appear near the base of some columns — possibly Cretaceous or Kazanian [uncertain]

The columns show **thickening and thinning of individual beds** across the section, indicating lateral facies changes and structural controls on deposition.

### Correlation Lines
**Diagonal/horizontal correlation lines** connect equivalent stratigraphic horizons between borehole columns. These are hand-drawn in the Soviet style and show:
- Some horizons are **continuous and parallel** — indicating laterally stable beds
- Others are **divergent or wedging** — indicating facies changes or unconformities
- A notable **unconformity or marker bed** appears approximately mid-section in several columns, with correlation lines converging

### Structural Markers
Roman numerals **IV, VII, K** (or similar) are annotated on certain columns mid-section, likely denoting **numbered productive horizons or marker beds** (реперные горизонты) used for correlation in the field.
